title: paspalum decumbens
intro: Paspalum decumbens, also known as buffalo grass, is a perennial warm-season grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Paspalum decumbens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Paspalum decumbens can be made into a tea, infused in oil, or applied topically as a paste to harness its medicinal properties.
